title: Geometric Deep Learning for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: This survey offers a comprehensive overview of learning-based methods in computer-aided design across various categories, including similarity analysis and retrieval, 2D and 3D CAD model synthesis, and point cloud-based CAD reconstruction. It covers geometric deep learning techniques applied to B-rep, mesh, point cloud, and voxel representations.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of geometric DL methods for CAD covering retrieval, synthesis, reconstruction, and segmentation. Organizes by task and representation type. Most directly comparable existing survey to our work.

title: Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: While substantial research has been conducted on LLMs in various fields, a comprehensive review focusing on their integration with Computer-Aided Design (CAD) remains notably absent. CAD is the industry standard for 3D modeling and plays a vital role in the design and development of products across different industries. This survey provides a comprehensive review of LLM applications in CAD.
method_summary: First survey specifically focused on LLM+CAD integration. Covers text-to-CAD, code generation, VLM-based approaches, and LLM-driven design agents. Key comparison target for our Table 1 as it focuses on the LLM dimension we also cover.

title: 3D Shape Generation: A Survey
abstract: This survey provides a comprehensive overview of the current state-of-the-art in 3D shape generation, organizing the discussion around three core components: shape representations, generative modeling techniques, and conditioning mechanisms. It covers point clouds, meshes, implicit functions, and parametric CAD representations.
method_summary: Broad 3D shape generation survey organized by representation and generation paradigm. Covers diffusion, autoregressive, GAN, VAE, and flow-based methods. Overlaps with our Sec 5 but lacks CAD-specific depth on B-rep, sequences, and manufacturing.

title: Advances in 3D Generation: A Survey
abstract: This survey aims to introduce the fundamental methodologies of 3D generation methods and establish a structured roadmap, encompassing 3D representation, generation methods, datasets, and corresponding applications.
method_summary: Broad survey of 3D generation covering NeRF, 3DGS, diffusion-based, and feed-forward methods. Focuses on general 3D content creation rather than engineering CAD. Useful comparison for our Table 1 to show our CAD-specific differentiation.

title: Diffusion Models in 3D Vision: A Survey
abstract: Diffusion models, originally designed for 2D generative tasks, offer the potential for more flexible, probabilistic approaches that can better capture the variability and uncertainty present in real-world 3D data. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of diffusion models applied to 3D vision tasks.
method_summary: Survey of diffusion models for 3D vision covering generation, reconstruction, completion, and editing. Relevant to our Sec 5 (3D generation) but does not address CAD-specific representations or manufacturing constraints.

title: Advancing Additive Manufacturing through Deep Learning: A Comprehensive Review of Current Progress and Future Challenges
abstract: Additive manufacturing (AM) has already proved itself to be the potential alternative to widely-used subtractive manufacturing due to its extraordinary capacity of manufacturing highly customized products. This review covers deep learning applications in AM including defect detection, process monitoring, quality prediction, and design optimization.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of DL in additive manufacturing covering process monitoring, defect detection, quality prediction, and generative design for AM. Relevant comparison for our Sec 8 (manufacturing) and Table 1.

title: A Survey On Text-to-3D Contents Generation In The Wild
abstract: In this survey, we conduct an in-depth investigation of the latest text-to-3D creation methods. We provide a comprehensive background on text-to-3D creation, including discussions on datasets employed, evaluation metrics, and the fundamental generative models utilized.
method_summary: Survey of text-to-3D generation methods covering SDS-based optimization, feed-forward generation, and multi-view approaches. Focuses on general 3D content (NeRF/mesh) rather than parametric CAD. Comparison target for Table 1.

title: Applications of Artificial Intelligence in the AEC Industry: A Review
venue: Journal of Asian Architecture and Building Engineering
abstract: In recent years, with the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI) technology, AI has been successfully applied across various domains within the construction sector. This paper reviews AI applications in architecture, engineering, and construction including BIM automation, structural design, and construction management.
method_summary: Review of AI in AEC covering BIM automation, generative architectural design, structural optimization, and construction robotics. Relevant for our coverage of AEC domain in Sec 1 and comparison in Table 1.

title: A Survey on Deep Learning in 3D CAD Reconstruction
abstract: This survey reviews deep learning methods for 3D CAD reconstruction from various inputs including point clouds, images, and sketches. It covers methods for B-rep recovery, parametric model inference, and sketch-based modeling.
method_summary: Survey focused specifically on DL-based CAD reconstruction (reverse engineering). Covers point-to-CAD, image-to-CAD, and sketch-to-CAD pipelines. Narrower scope than our survey but directly relevant to Sec 5/6.

title: LLM4CAD: Multi-Modal Large Language Models for 3D Computer-Aided Design Generation
abstract: Multi-Modal Large Language Models for 3D Computer-Aided Design Generation. Proceedings of the ASME 2024 International Design Engineering Technical Conferences.
method_summary: Early work on multi-modal LLMs for CAD generation presented at ASME IDETC. Demonstrates integration of visual and textual modalities for CAD model generation using LLMs.
